LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANT ZHONG LUN WU'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND PURSUANT TO CCP 430.10(E). THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT ("FACC") DOES NOT ALLEGE THE ELEMENTS OF FRAUD WITH THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY. (SEE LAZAR V. SUPERIOR COURT (1996) 12 CAL.4TH 631.) THE FACC DOES NOT ALLEGE THERE WAS A MISREPRESENTATION, GIVEN THAT THE ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATION IS THAT "YU'S ELECTRICIAN LICENSE WAS NEEDED FOR ELECTRICAL WORK ON HIN DUN KONG'S HOUSE," AND YET THE FOLLOWING SENTENCE STATES THAT THE LICENSE WAS IN FACT NEEDED BECAUSE WU SIGNED A CONTRACT WITH KONG USING THAT LICENSE. (FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT, 10.) IT IS ALSO UNCLEAR HOW WU MISREPRESENTING TO YU THAT HE NEEDED HIS ELECTRICIAN LICENSE CAUSED YU TO BE SUED FOR UNLICENSED CONSTRUCTION. (ID.) THE FACC ALSO DOES NOT STATE FACTS TO SUPPORT A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR EQUITABLE INDEMNITY, BECAUSE THE FACC DOES NOT ALLEGE FACTS THAT GIVE RISE TO TORTIOUS CONDUCT ON CROSS-DEFENDANT'S PART. (SEE BAILEY V. SAFEWAY, INC. (2011) 199 CAL.APP.4TH 206, 217. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT ZHONG WU'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD, THE SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT ("SACC") IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. (CCP SEC. 338.) CROSS-COMPLAINANT IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS, IF HE CAN IN GOOD FAITH, THAT ESTABLISH DELAYED DISCOVERY, AS ARGUED IN CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S OPPOSITION BRIEF. AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INDEMNIFICATION AND APPORTIONMENT OF FAULT, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE THE SACC DOES NOT ALLEGE A BASIS FOR TORT-LIABILITY AGAINST CROSS-DEFENDANT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED. (302)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders balance of case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. JUDGE JOHN K. STEWART; Clerk MOlopernes; Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-21-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-03-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-01-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT ZHONG WU'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to the third cause of action for fraud, the Second Amended Cross-Complaint ("SACC") is barred by the statute of limitations. (CCP sec. 338.) Cross-Complainant is given leave to amend to allege facts, if he can in good faith, that establish delayed discovery, as argued in Cross-Complainant's opposition brief. As to the first and second causes of action for indemnification and apportionment of fault, the demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend because the SACC does not allege a basis for tort-liability against Cross-Defendant. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-01-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT ZHONG WU'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is denied. Cross-Complainant was granted leave of court to file a second amended cross-complaint on February 3, 2014 when the Court sustained Cross-Defendant's demurrer with ten days leave to amend. (CCP sec. 428.50(c).) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-27-2014 Continued To Jul-21-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Feb-27-2014 Text Ruling

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-03-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS DEFENDANT ZHONG LUN WU's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Sustained with 10 days leave to amend pursuant to CCP 430.10(e). The First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") does not allege the elements of fraud with the requisite specificity. (See Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631.) The FACC does not allege there was a misrepresentation, given that the alleged misrepresentation is that "Yu's electrician license was needed for electrical work on Hin Dun Kong's house," and yet the following sentence states that the license was in fact needed because Wu signed a contract with Kong using that license. (First Amended Cross-Complaint, 10.) It is also unclear how Wu misrepresenting to Yu that he needed his electrician license caused Yu to be sued for unlicensed construction. (Id.) The FACC also does not state facts to support a cause of action for equitable indemnity, because the FACC does not allege facts that give rise to tortious conduct on Cross-Defendant's part. (See Bailey v. Safeway, Inc. (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 206, 217. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: A. James Robertson, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/AR)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-09-2014 Continued To Feb-03-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT ZHONG LUN WU's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS Continued to February 3, 2014 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 9, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT KE ZHU, EAST RAINBOW CONSTRUCTION INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part. Cross-Complainant has leave to amend to attach exhibit C to the cross-complaint. Zhu and East Rainbow's demurrer to the fraud cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to allege a basis for liability, e.g., conspirator, agent, aider and abettor. The reference to B&P Code 7114 is misplaced because that is a disciplinary statute. The Court overrules East Rainbow's demurrer to the second cause of action for violation of B&P Code 7031. Cross-Complainant sufficiently alleges that East Rainbow was unlicensed and received Cross-complainant's money. The Court sustains Zhu's demurrer to the second cause of action without leave to amend. The cross-complaint alleges that Zhu was licensed and there is no allegation that he received cross-complainant's money. order signed after hearing. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
